howItWorks.intro: This savings calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: P = initial deposit, r = monthly rate, n = months, PMT = monthly contribution
howItWorks.outro: Tax laws and financial markets change frequently. Verify current rates with your financial institution.

faq.question: How does compound interest work?
faq.answer: Your interest earns interest. With monthly compounding, each month's interest is added to the balance and earns interest in subsequent months. This creates exponential growth over time.

faq.question: What return rate should I use?
faq.answer: High-yield savings: 4-5%. Bonds: 3-5%. Stock market average: 7-10%. Use a conservative estimate for planning purposes.

faq.question: How much should I save each month?
faq.answer: The 50/30/20 rule suggests saving 20% of after-tax income. But any amount helps — even $100/month becomes $16,470 in 10 years at 5%.

faq.question: Is this guaranteed?
faq.answer: Savings accounts and CDs have FDIC insurance up to $250,000. Investment returns are not guaranteed and can fluctuate significantly.

howWeCalculate.intro: This savings calculator uses established formulas and current data to provide accurate estimates.

howWeCalculate.disclaimer: These calculations are estimates based on standard formulas. For critical decisions, always consult a qualified professional.

whenToUse.intro: This savings calculator is designed for anyone who needs quick, reliable calculations without complex spreadsheets or professional consultations.
scenarios: When planning major financial decisions like buying a home, investing, or taking on debt
When comparing different financial products or scenarios side by side
When building a budget or projecting future savings and expenses
When you want to verify a quote or estimate from a financial advisor or lender
When teaching or learning about personal finance and compound growth
